title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Changed-History Lab
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a signed-off release evidence record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Release v3.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. steps.why: Retrieving Signature Trust through the concrete a signed-off release evidence record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
